member_findings.title: Remove committed AI conversation transcript
member_findings.impact: The new repository artifact publishes an internal AI development transcript, including assistant reasoning, model/usage metadata, and tool-call context. That information is not needed to build or validate the feature and becomes permanently visible anywhere the repository is mirrored or packaged.
member_findings.fix: Delete the .conversations JSONL file from the PR and keep AI/tool transcripts out of source control; if any context is useful for reviewers, summarize it in the PR description without hidden reasoning or tool metadata.

clusters.title: Remove generated conversation transcript from the product diff

member_findings.title: Remove generated conversation transcript from the product diff
member_findings.impact: The PR checks in a 31-line AI conversation transcript that is neither source, tests, documentation, nor repo context. Future maintainers have to decide whether it is authoritative design history, test data, or accidental debug output, and it can drift independently from the implementation.
member_findings.fix: Drop the .conversations JSONL file from the PR. If the design rationale is useful, move a concise, source-backed summary into the existing repo context or the PR description instead.

clusters.title: Avoid a second payload_info scan on URMA Get

member_findings.title: Avoid a second payload_info scan on URMA Get
member_findings.impact: Every URMA Get response with payload metadata now calls HasUrmaTcpFallbackPayload before FillUrmaBuffer, and FillUrmaBuffer already walks the same payload_info list to skip TCP fallback entries. Batch Get allows up to 10000 keys, so successful remote Get adds a full extra protobuf repeated-field pass on a foreground hot path.
member_findings.fix: Fold fallback detection into the existing FillUrmaBuffer pass, for example by returning or filling a bool that records whether any payload_info entry already had part_index entries, then call RecordUrmaDataPlaneResult from that result instead of scanning payload_info separately.

clusters.title: Add negative coverage for the new URMA failover gflag validators

member_findings.title: Add negative coverage for the new URMA failover gflag validators
member_findings.impact: The PR adds validation branches that reject non-finite or out-of-range success-rate ratios and a zero min-sample count, but the added UT/ST coverage only exercises accepted values and hot updates. A future regression could accidentally accept NaN, ratios outside [0,1], or min_sample_count=0 and the failover control loop would run with invalid configuration without a failing test.
member_findings.fix: Add a focused gflag/validator unit test that asserts urma_failover_success_rate_ratio accepts 0.0, 0.5, and 1.0 but rejects NaN, infinity, negative values, and values greater than 1.0, and asserts urma_failover_min_sample_count rejects 0 while accepting positive values.

clusters.title: Document the URMA failover callback return contract consistently

member_findings.title: Document the URMA failover callback return contract consistently
member_findings.impact: The interface comment says the callback returns true when the worker switch succeeds, but the installed callback returns true when it successfully submits asynchronous switch work and reports the real switch result later through FinishUrmaDataPlaneSwitchAttempt. This mismatch makes the callback contract easy to misuse in future implementations.
member_findings.fix: Change the comment to say the callback returns whether asynchronous switch work was submitted, and keep the switch outcome contract on FinishUrmaDataPlaneSwitchAttempt.

clusters.title: Hide the test-only tracker accessor from the production interface

member_findings.title: Hide the test-only tracker accessor from the production interface
member_findings.impact: GetCurrentSampleCountForTest is exposed in the production tracker header even though its comment marks it as test only. That makes an internal counter layout part of the visible class surface and invites future production callers to couple to tracker internals.
member_findings.fix: Move this accessor behind a test-only compile guard or expose test inspection through a friend test fixture. If production diagnostics need this value, rename it to a non-test API and document the supported contract.
